news.title: CPVA Partners with CVASU for Research Initiative
news.summary: CPVA signed an MoU with Chittagong Veterinary and Animal Sciences University to collaborate on research into livestock diseases prevalent in the Chattogram region.
news.content:
CPVA signed an MoU with Chittagong Veterinary and Animal Sciences University (CVASU) on 5th June 2026 to collaborate on research into livestock diseases prevalent in the Chattogram region. The partnership will include joint research projects, student internships, and knowledge-sharing seminars.

The MoU was signed by CPVA President Dr. Imdadul Haque Bhuiyan and CVASU Vice-Chancellor Prof. Dr. A.S. Mahfuzul Bari in the presence of senior faculty members and executive committee members of CPVA.

This is a major milestone for CPVA as it formalizes our long-standing relationship with the university's veterinary faculty.

news.title: Free Vaccination Camp Serves 200+ Animals
news.summary: CPVA's free anti-rabies vaccination camp in collaboration with Chattogram City Corporation vaccinated over 200 dogs and cats in a single day.
news.content:
CPVA's free anti-rabies vaccination camp, organized in collaboration with Chattogram City Corporation on World Veterinary Day, vaccinated over 200 dogs and cats in a single day at MA Aziz Stadium.

The camp was led by 15 CPVA member veterinarians who volunteered their time. CPVA also distributed free pet care pamphlets and conducted an awareness session on responsible pet ownership.

CPVA President Dr. Imdadul Haque Bhuiyan thanked all volunteers and announced that such camps will be organized quarterly going forward.

news.title: CPVA Calls for Stricter Drug Regulation
news.summary: CPVA executive committee has issued a statement calling on the government to tighten regulations on the sale of veterinary drugs.
news.content:
CPVA executive committee has issued a formal statement calling on the government to tighten regulations on the sale and distribution of veterinary drugs in Chattogram division.

The statement highlights concerns about the misuse of antibiotics and growth promoters, and the rising incidence of antimicrobial resistance in local livestock populations.

The association has offered to work with the Department of Livestock Services on drafting new regulations and conducting training programs for drug retailers.

news.title: New Executive Committee Takes Office
news.summary: The newly elected executive committee of CPVA for 2026-2027 officially took office on 1st January 2026.
news.content:
The newly elected executive committee of CPVA for the 2026-2027 term officially took office on 1st January 2026. Dr. Imdadul Haque Bhuiyan was re-elected as President.

The new committee has identified the following priorities for the year: (1) increasing membership by 25%, (2) launching the Continuing Education Program, (3) strengthening the complaint resolution system, and (4) improving member benefits and discounts.
